barRaise.topFinding: The code is healthy; the commit discipline is the binding constraint, and it slipped again. Yesterday's review ended one sentence from a clean bill: the next review is one git commit and one git rm --cached away from healthy. Neither happened. HEAD is unchanged at v0.57.10 (2026-06-11), the entire v0.58.0 MINOR (Sonic Pressure wave-tier scaling plus convoy resupply) still sits as six uncommitted working-tree files, and the five tracked .bak files the changelog claims are gone are still in the index. The v0.58.0 code itself is correct and well-commented. This is the build-then-sit pattern reasserting, now aged a full review cycle. Nothing here gates the milestone, but the same two MEDIUM housekeeping actions are now verbatim carryovers, unactioned.
